cases.title: East Africa (The EAC)
cases.description: Home to the Great Lakes and the Source of the Nile.
steps: Countries: Uganda, Kenya, Tanzania, Rwanda, Burundi, South Sudan, and DRC.
Organization: These countries form the East African Community (EAC).
Special Features: Contains Lake Victoria (largest in Africa), Mt. Kilimanjaro (highest peak), and the Source of the River Nile.
It is crossed by the Equator.

cases.title: North Africa (The Sahara)
cases.description: The arid region bordering the Mediterranean Sea.
steps: Countries: Egypt, Libya, Tunisia, Algeria, Morocco, Western Sahara, Sudan.
Special Features: Home to the vast Sahara Desert and the Atlas Mountains (Morocco).
Water: The River Nile flows north into the Mediterranean Sea here.
Climate: Mostly hot and dry (Desert climate), with Mediterranean climate on the coast.

cases.title: West Africa (ECOWAS)
cases.description: The bulge of Africa, rich in population and history.
steps: Countries: Nigeria, Ghana, Senegal, Mali, Niger, Cote D'Ivoire, Benin, Togo, Liberia, Sierra Leone, Guinea, Gambia, Burkina Faso.
Organization: Most belong to ECOWAS.
Special Features: The River Niger forms a delta here. It is crossed by the Prime Meridian (Ghana/Mali).
Fact: Nigeria is the most populated country in Africa.

cases.title: The Horn of Africa
cases.description: The peninsula that juts into the Indian Ocean.
steps: Countries: Somalia, Ethiopia, Eritrea, Djibouti.
Shape: It looks like the horn of a Rhinoceros.
Special Features: Contains the Ethiopian Highlands. It is very dry and prone to drought.
Ethiopia: The only African country never colonized.

cases.title: Central Africa (The Rainforest)
cases.description: The heart of the continent, covered in thick forests.
steps: Countries: Dem. Rep. of Congo (DRC), Congo Republic, Central African Republic (CAR), Gabon, Cameroon, Chad.
Climate: Hot and Wet (Equatorial).
Special Features: The Congo Rainforest (2nd largest in the world) and the mighty Congo River.
It is rich in minerals but has thick vegetation.

cases.title: Southern Africa (SADC)
steps: Countries: South Africa, Namibia, Botswana, Zimbabwe, Zambia, Malawi, Mozambique, Lesotho, Eswatini.
Organization: SADC (Southern African Development Community).
Special Features: Kalahari Desert, Drakensberg Mountains, and Cape Agulhas.
Known for Gold and Diamond mining.
